# plan9obj

```go
import "debug/plan9obj"
```

Package plan9obj implements access to Plan 9 a.out object files.

### Security

This package is not designed to be hardened against adversarial inputs, and is outside the scope of https://go.dev/security/policy. In particular, only basic validation is done when parsing object files. As such, care should be taken when parsing untrusted inputs, as parsing malformed files may consume significant resources, or cause panics.

## Constants

<a name="Magic64"></a>

```go
const (
    Magic64 = 0x8000 // 64-bit expanded header

    Magic386   = (4*11+0)*11 + 7
    MagicAMD64 = (4*26+0)*26 + 7 + Magic64
    MagicARM   = (4*20+0)*20 + 7
)
```

## Variables

<a name="ErrNoSymbols"></a>ErrNoSymbols is returned by [File.Symbols](<#File.Symbols>) if there is no such section in the File.

```go
var ErrNoSymbols = errors.New("no symbol section")
```

<a name="File"></a>

## type [File](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L38-L42>)

A File represents an open Plan 9 a.out file.

```go
type File struct {
    FileHeader
    Sections []*Section
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewFile"></a>

### func [NewFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L141>)

```go
func NewFile(r io.ReaderAt) (*File, error)
```

NewFile creates a new [File](<#File>) for accessing a Plan 9 binary in an underlying reader. The Plan 9 binary is expected to start at position 0 in the ReaderAt.

<a name="Open"></a>

### func [Open](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L104>)

```go
func Open(name string) (*File, error)
```

Open opens the named file using [os.Open](<https://pkg.go.dev/os/#Open>) and prepares it for use as a Plan 9 a.out binary.

<a name="File.Close"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Close](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L121>)

```go
func (f *File) Close() error
```

Close closes the [File](<#File>). If the [File](<#File>) was created using [NewFile](<#NewFile>) directly instead of [Open](<#Open>), Close has no effect.

<a name="File.Section"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Section](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L333>)

```go
func (f *File) Section(name string) *Section
```

Section returns a section with the given name, or nil if no such section exists.

<a name="File.Symbols"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Symbols](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L317>)

```go
func (f *File) Symbols() ([]Sym, error)
```

Symbols returns the symbol table for f.

<a name="FileHeader"></a>

## type [FileHeader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L28-L35>)

A FileHeader represents a Plan 9 a.out file header.

```go
type FileHeader struct {
    Magic       uint32
    Bss         uint32
    Entry       uint64
    PtrSize     int
    LoadAddress uint64
    HdrSize     uint64
}
```

<a name="Section"></a>

## type [Section](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L54-L65>)

A Section represents a single section in a Plan 9 a.out file.

```go
type Section struct {
    SectionHeader

    // Embed ReaderAt for ReadAt method.
    // Do not embed SectionReader directly
    // to avoid having Read and Seek.
    // If a client wants Read and Seek it must use
    // Open() to avoid fighting over the seek offset
    // with other clients.
    io.ReaderAt
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Section.Data"></a>

### func \(\*Section\) [Data](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L68>)

```go
func (s *Section) Data() ([]byte, error)
```

Data reads and returns the contents of the Plan 9 a.out section.

<a name="Section.Open"></a>

### func \(\*Section\) [Open](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L73>)

```go
func (s *Section) Open() io.ReadSeeker
```

Open returns a new ReadSeeker reading the Plan 9 a.out section.

<a name="SectionHeader"></a>

## type [SectionHeader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L47-L51>)

A SectionHeader represents a single Plan 9 a.out section header. This structure doesn't exist on\-disk, but eases navigation through the object file.

```go
type SectionHeader struct {
    Name   string
    Size   uint32
    Offset uint32
}
```

<a name="Sym"></a>

## type [Sym](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/debug/plan9obj/file.go#L76-L80>)

A Symbol represents an entry in a Plan 9 a.out symbol table section.

```go
type Sym struct {
    Value uint64
    Type  rune
    Name  string
}
```
